completed_tasks.name: Rename implementation-branded ingestion components to domain-oriented names
Promote Spring AI readers to the primary ingestion path and constrain Tika fallback
Move chunking toward token splitting while preserving metadata and audit fields

decisions.decision: Spring AI ETL/readers are now the default ingestion pipeline for covered formats
decisions.rationale: Repo-owned code remains the policy and control layer while Spring AI handles covered reader and chunking behavior

decisions.decision: Ingestion naming is domain-oriented instead of implementation-branded
decisions.rationale: Responsibility-based names are easier to maintain than library-branded names

decisions.decision: Chunking provenance is persisted on KbSourceVersion
decisions.rationale: Admins need parser and chunking auditability for ingestion review

next_action: Begin the next planned workflow after Phase 01.1 completion, likely Phase 2 grounded legal Q&A work or formal phase transition updates.
context_notes: Plan 01.1-04 has been implemented and committed in 146a22c. Parser selection is resolver-based, Spring AI is primary for covered formats, token chunking is active, and chunking provenance is persisted. Remaining work is workflow and phase progression rather than ingestion refactor implementation.
